title: Xi'an
html:
Xi'an

The ancient Chinese capital — terminus of the Silk Road, home of the Terracotta Warriors — and one of the three pole-cities of the China chapter alongside Shanghai (the base) and Chengdu (the Sichuan side trip). Xi'an enters the corpus first in 2024 09 05 shanghai sidequests as a bucket-list day-trip pulled out of Shanghai: "Terra Cotta Warriors (Xi'an day trip)" sitting alongside the Great Wall, the Tesla Gigafactory tour, and a Wynton Marsalis Lincoln Jazz Center evening — the early-China shape of Shanghai-as-base, China-as-radius.

By Nov 7 2024 (2024 11 07 xi an) Yahir has actually committed: a tight 3-day itinerary that uses the same template he developed for Chengdu in 2024 09 25 things to do in chengdu (sights / food / nightlife, broken out by day). Day 1: Terracotta Warriors, Huaqing Palace, the Muslim Quarter + Great Mosque. Day 2: Shaanxi Museum (free, arrive early), Big Wild Goose Pagoda, the ancient city wall. Day 3: Yongxing Fang food street, Xi'an Brewery. Food hit-list is canonical Shaanxi: rou jia mo (the "Chinese hamburger"), biangbiang noodles, and yang rou paomo (flatbread crumbled into mutton soup). Nightlife: Defu Alley, Jiefang Road, the brewery.

Xi'an matters in the graph for two reasons. First, it adds the Shaanxi cuisine pole to a corpus that previously only had Sichuan for Chinese regional eating — the food-as-place-anchor pattern Yahir uses everywhere from his Corvallis date-vault to the Vietnam route prep. Second, it sits chronologically between the 2024 10 22 valeria visit and 2024 11 28 leading thoughts about 6 month adventure notes, meaning Xi'an is one of the last big sights he squeezed in as the China chapter visibly winds down — a marker that he was front-loading the historical-imperial side of China before leaving in December (2024 12 14 departing china). The 2024 08 14 travel occurrences travel-stories catalog is curiously silent on Xi'an, which suggests either the trip was too smooth to make the mishap-bank or it's still pending an entry.
